info:
These are the pure interrogatives
1. qui — “who”
Qui est là ? → Who is there?
2. quand — “when”
quand asks about time, but not about the name of a time-unit.
When something happens
When it starts, ends, occurs, etc.
Quand arrives-tu ? → When do you arrive?
If you say quelle heure, that includes the noun heure, so the pure interrogative is just quelle.
3. où — “where”
Où vas-tu ? → Where are you going?
It can also appear in expanded forms like:
où est-ce que…
4. pourquoi — “why”
Pourquoi es-tu en retard ? → Why are you late?
5. que / qu’est-ce que — “what” (object)
French often requires a heavier structure than English for “what.”
que - The formal inversion form:
Que fais-tu ? → What are you doing?
qu’est-ce que - The natural, everyday spoken-French form:
Qu’est-ce que tu fais ? → What are you doing?
This looks long, but functionally it is just “what” as the object of a verb
6. de quoi — “what (about)”
Use de quoi when English uses what after a preposition.
De quoi parles-tu ? → What are you talking about?
Here, quoi appears because French uses quoi after prepositions.
7. quel / quelle — “which / what (with a noun)”
These are interrogative adjectives, so normally they require a noun:
Quelle musique ? → What music?
Quel film ? → Which movie?
Depending on gender and number:
native_answer: who is she? the teacher
answer: qui est-elle ? la professeure
audio_dir: qui est-elle ? la professeure

audio_dir: Pourquoi le sol est-il mouillé ? Le seau est tombé

info:
These are the pure interrogatives
1. qui — “who”
Qui est là ? → Who is there?
2. quand — “when”
quand asks about time, but not about the name of a time-unit.
When something happens
When it starts, ends, occurs, etc.
Quand arrives-tu ? → When do you arrive?
If you say quelle heure, that includes the noun heure, so the pure interrogative is just quelle.
3. où — “where”
Où vas-tu ? → Where are you going?
It can also appear in expanded forms like:
où est-ce que…
4. pourquoi — “why”
Pourquoi es-tu en retard ? → Why are you late?
5. que / qu’est-ce que — “what” (object)
French often requires a heavier structure than English for “what.”
que - The formal inversion form:
Que fais-tu ? → What are you doing?
qu’est-ce que - The natural, everyday spoken-French form:
Qu’est-ce que tu fais ? → What are you doing?
This looks long, but functionally it is just “what” as the object of a verb
6. de quoi — “what (about)”
Use de quoi when English uses what after a preposition.
De quoi parles-tu ? → What are you talking about?
Here, quoi appears because French uses quoi after prepositions.
7. quel / quelle — “which / what (with a noun)”
These are interrogative adjectives, so normally they require a noun:
Quelle musique ? → What music?
Quel film ? → Which movie?
Depending on gender and number:
native_answer: why are the students worried? a test
prompt: ______ les étudiants sont-ils inquiets ? un test
answer: pourquoi les étudiants sont-ils inquiets ? un test
